title: Essential Makeup Fundamentals Every Artist Must Master
author: Author Name

sections.heading: Build Your Base With Precision
paragraphs: Treat the base as foundational work. Skin prep comes first, the formulation lasts, and precision defines the final look. Understand your base before you layer artistry. Cleanser → tone → prep → primer → foundation → set with intention, for a natural, flattering finish.
sections.outcomeText: A controlled, even surface that doesn’t collapse under light or wear, helping your makeup stay seamless from ceremony to photos.

sections.heading: Hygiene and Workflow Discipline
paragraphs: Specialists don’t just create beauty—they maintain trust. Work with clean tools, proper product handling, and a consistent workflow. Keep disposables ready, sanitize between clients, and set up your station so you never scramble mid-application.
sections.outcomeText: Predictable, safe, and efficient sessions—so you stay focused on your artistry while clients feel comfortable and cared for.

sections.heading: Final Checks Before Photos
paragraphs: The last 10 minutes matter: check balance in natural light, ensure photos capture texture correctly, and refine placement around eyes, cheeks, and lips. Smooth any edges, blot excess, and confirm that your base still looks fresh in close-ups.
sections.outcomeText: A camera-ready finish that looks polished in real life and in every angle—without feeling heavy or overdone.

title: 9 Questions to your Bridal Makeup Artists
author: Author Name

sections.heading: Overview
paragraphs: From booking timelines to trials and wedding-day prep, this page answers the most common questions brides ask so choosing your bridal makeup team feels simpler.
sections.outcomeText: A clearer, calmer process from first enquiry to wedding day.
